title: HPKE, Hybrid Encryption, and Envelope Systems · Language Lens: Crypto Go Rewrap Without Reencrypt
summary: A sharing service encrypts one payload separately for every recipient, stores long-lived data keys in metadata, and invents a hybrid construction without binding recipient or suite context. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the rewrap without reencrypt increment in multi-recipient envelope encryption format. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed…
objectives: Rotate recipient or KEK protection by authenticated rewrapping while preserving payload identity, audit, rollback, and revocation limits.
Bind each recipient, wrapped key, suite, object version, and payload digest so entries cannot be swapped or replayed.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 3874 during a credential migration window. Build deterministic Go evidence to bind an HPKE or hybrid envelope to recipient, object, suite, payload, and audited rewrap state; preserve accessible status, non-color meaning, keyboard operation, error recovery, and secret-safe diagnostics; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
